action_level_closure_policy: All physical/action proof gates and action-derived provenance must pass.
allowed_claims: BHSM includes a focused CP O_int field/action construction attempt.
The sprint distinguishes phase attachment from standalone field/action interaction structure.
The sprint evaluates field representation, Lorentz structure, gauge admissibility, coupling normalization, action/source term, production eligibility, and runtime export eligibility.
A symbolic candidate is not action-level closure.
conditional_author_axiom_policy: Enabled author templates are capped at DERIVED_CONDITIONAL_AUTHOR_AXIOM.
forbidden_claims: The CP O_int field/action sprint empirically validates BHSM.
CP holonomy alone is a standalone interaction theorem.
CKM/PMNS phase attachment alone proves standalone O_int.
A conditional author axiom is action-level closure.
Reference or PDG values are theorem inputs.
FeynRules/UFO/MadGraph readiness is achieved without runtime validation.
policy_name: BHSM CP O_int Sprint C Claim Policy
production_eligibility_policy: Production eligibility requires physical factors, action source, production callable, provenance, and tests.
reference_value_policy: Reference, PDG, W-anchor, and calibration values are never theorem inputs.
runtime_gate_policy: FeynRules, UFO, and MadGraph require separate live validation.
symbolic_candidate_policy: AVAILABLE_SYMBOLIC_CANDIDATE localizes factors but does not satisfy action-level proof gates.
